tabs.title: Who We Are
tabs.content:
Manchester School of Business and Technology (MSBT) is a forward-thinking provider of business, management and technology education, created to make high-quality professional study genuinely accessible. We bring together academically rigorous content and practical, work-ready skills so that our learners leave us better equipped for the careers they want and the responsibilities they will hold.

Flexibility sits at the heart of our model. Our programmes are delivered online and structured around the realities of adult life, allowing learners to balance study with work, family and other commitments while still progressing at a pace that suits them. From a first step into management to advanced strategic study, MSBT offers a clear, supported pathway at every level.

We are proud to serve a diverse, international community of learners. Different experiences and perspectives strengthen the way our students learn from one another and mirror the connected, global environment in which modern organisations operate. Across everything we do, we are guided by a simple set of commitments: quality, fairness, integrity, and the long-term success of every learner who studies with us.

tabs.title: Our Vision and Mission
tabs.content:
Vision

Our vision is for Manchester School of Business and Technology to be recognised as a leading provider of business, management and technology education, known for the quality of our teaching, the flexibility of our learning and the achievements of our graduates.

Mission

Our mission is to widen access to professional education and to develop confident, capable and ethical professionals. We do this by delivering learner-centred programmes that combine academic depth with practical relevance, by removing unnecessary barriers to study through flexible online delivery, and by supporting each learner — with care and high expectations — to fulfil their potential and make a positive contribution in their workplace and community.

tabs.title: Our Objectives
tabs.content:
To turn our vision into reality, Manchester School of Business and Technology works towards a clear set of objectives:

Deliver and continuously improve excellence in teaching, learning and academic support.

Attract and welcome a diverse community of learners from across the United Kingdom and around the world.

Widen participation by removing barriers to study, including through flexible online delivery and targeted support for learners who would not otherwise have the opportunity.

Develop graduates who are competent, innovative and enterprising, and who are ready to succeed in employment or further study.

Maintain strong governance, sound management and responsible use of our resources.

Build lasting, mutually beneficial relationships with employers and with local, national and international partners.

tabs.title: Governance and Leadership
tabs.content:
Clear and transparent governance underpins the way Manchester School of Business and Technology is run. Our structure establishes well-defined lines of accountability and ensures consistent oversight of academic standards, conduct, fairness and risk across the institution.

Governing Body

The Governing Body provides strategic leadership and direction. Its core responsibilities include:

Setting and approving the institution’s long-term strategy.

Approving institutional policies and the annual budget.

Delegating appropriate authority to committees and monitoring progress against our strategic aims.

Reviewing institutional performance and holding leadership to account.

Setting expected standards of behaviour and promoting shared values, acting at all times with integrity.

Taking informed, transparent decisions and overseeing the management of risk.

Developing the board’s own capability so that it remains effective.

Ensuring the institution operates within all applicable legal and regulatory requirements.

Academic Board

The Academic Board is the senior academic authority of Manchester School of Business and Technology. Reporting to the Governing Body, it has oversight of all academic matters across our business, health and social care, and psychology provision, including academic standards, the quality of learning, teaching and assessment, and the approval and review of programmes. It is supported by the standing committees set out below.

Quality and Standards Committee

The Quality and Standards Committee monitors academic performance and the standards of the awards our learners work towards across every subject area, recommending improvements to the Academic Board so that quality is maintained and continuously enhanced.

Learning, Teaching and Assessment Committee

This committee oversees the design and delivery of teaching and assessment, including internal moderation and standardisation, so that assessment practice and the learner experience are consistent, fair and effective across every programme and level.

Assessment and Awards Board (Board of Examiners)

The Assessment and Awards Board considers and confirms learners’ internal assessment outcomes, ensuring that marking is fair and consistent before results are submitted for external verification by the relevant awarding organisation.

Research and Research Ethics Committee

This committee oversees the research elements of our programmes — including the research methods and research projects within our business, health and social care, and psychology qualifications — and provides ethical review and approval, with particular care where study involves people or sensitive subject matter.

Safeguarding and Student Wellbeing Committee

The Safeguarding and Student Wellbeing Committee oversees our safeguarding, mental health and wellbeing arrangements. This is especially important across our health and social care and psychology provision, ensuring that learners are well supported and that safeguarding responsibilities are clearly understood and met.

Equality, Diversity and Inclusion Committee

This committee promotes equality, diversity and inclusion across the institution and within the curriculum, reflecting both our diverse international community of learners and the values that run through our health and social care provision.

Student Experience and Engagement Committee

The Student Experience and Engagement Committee provides a formal route for the student voice. It gathers and acts on learner feedback to improve the support, experience and outcomes of those who study with us.

Appeals Committee

The Appeals Committee convenes as required to consider and determine appeals relating to disciplinary, grievance, suspension and dismissal decisions, ensuring such matters are handled fairly and consistently.

Appeals and Complaints Panels

Panels are convened as needed to deal with academic appeals, enquiries against assessment results, and complaints. They provide a transparent and impartial route for learners and staff to have concerns considered without undue delay.

tabs.title: Quality Assurance and Risk Management
tabs.content:
Quality Assurance

Manchester School of Business and Technology maintains a framework of policies, processes and systems designed to assure the quality of our learning and teaching and the standards of the awards our learners work towards. Built on recognised principles of academic quality and on the relevant legal requirements, our framework ensures that what we deliver is rigorous, fair and regularly reviewed, with the learner experience at its centre.

Risk Management

We take an integrated, institution-wide approach to managing risk. Risks are identified by the staff and managers closest to our day-to-day operations, who understand them best. Operational risks are overseen by senior leadership and escalated to the Governing Body where necessary, while strategic risks are reviewed by the Governing Body on a regular basis. This ensures that the most significant risks to the institution and to our learners are identified, prioritised and managed on a continuing basis.

tabs.title: Student Support
tabs.content:
Manchester School of Business and Technology is committed to giving every learner the guidance and support they need to succeed. We do this by:

Providing clear, accurate and up-to-date information about the qualifications we offer.

Helping each learner choose the qualification that best matches their goals and experience.

Signposting learners to appropriate external support where a need falls outside what we can provide directly.

Setting out our fees clearly and transparently before enrolment.

Providing pre-course information and guidance in good time.

Ensuring our tutors, assessors and quality assurers are appropriately qualified and competent.

Making sure learners understand the assessment requirements of their qualification.

Ensuring all learners have fair access to assessment.

Explaining clearly what happens, and what support is available, where a learner has not yet demonstrated the required competence.

Making our appeals and complaints procedures easy to find and understand.

Keeping learners informed of the rules, regulations and policies that relate to their study, assessment and the quality assurance of their work.

Ensuring successful learners receive their certificates promptly.
